question_text: An electromagnet is used in a scrapyard crane to lift crushed cars. Which two factors would make the electromagnet stronger so it can lift heavier loads?
correct_answer: Increasing the current (or voltage) through the coil and/or increasing the number of coils (turns) in the electromagnet.
explanation: Both increasing current and increasing the number of coils increase the magnetic field strength. In a scrapyard, engineers would use both methods to make the electromagnet powerful enough to lift heavy metal scraps.
